Transformers: An Introduction

This article gives a friendly introduction to the transformer architecture used in modern large language models. We start with the attention mechanism and build up from there.

Self-attention

Self-attention is the core operation. Each token attends to every other token, weighted by a learned similarity. The output is a context-aware representation of the input sequence.

Multi-head attention runs several attention operations in parallel, each with its own learned projections. The heads can specialise on different relationships within the sequence.

Feed-forward layers

After attention, each position passes independently through a two-layer MLP. This adds non-linear capacity to the network without introducing further cross-token dependencies.

Residual connections and layer normalisation keep the gradients stable during training, which lets us stack many such blocks.
